title: Finding somewhere to live :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Finding somewhere to live
When choosing where to live, you need to think about what kind of accommodation you need short-term, if you want to rent or buy a home, and if you need to be close to schools, friends or family.
- Choosing where to live in New Zealand Where in New Zealand you choose to live might depend on your job or where you want to study. But other considerations may affect your decision.

## Choosing where to live in New Zealand
Where in New Zealand you choose to live might depend on your job or where you want to study. But other considerations may affect your decision.
- Hotels, motels and other places to live temporarily Unless you will be staying with family or have accommodation organised by an employer or education provider, you will need somewhere to live for the first few weeks while you look for something more permanent.

## Hotels, motels and other places to live temporarily
Unless you will be staying with family or have accommodation organised by an employer or education provider, you will need somewhere to live for the first few weeks while you look for something more permanent.
- Renting a place to live in New Zealand If you plan to rent property or live in shared accommodation in New Zealand, it is important to learn about your rights and responsibilities as a tenant, and those of the landlord.

## Renting a place to live in New Zealand
If you plan to rent property or live in shared accommodation in New Zealand, it is important to learn about your rights and responsibilities as a tenant, and those of the landlord.
- Buying property in New Zealand Find out who can buy residential property in New Zealand, how to find, buy or build a home, and what costs may be part of the process.

## Buying property in New Zealand
Find out who can buy residential property in New Zealand, how to find, buy or build a home, and what costs may be part of the process.
